refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these were separated into distinct sub-FRs. Additionally, the FR was kept focused on 'what' the system must do (derive requirements) without implying 'how' (methods or technologies).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can identify and specify performance requirements based solely on the worksite mission context without reference to implementation details.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall record performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.

candidate_dps: Define a performance requirements derivation process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets
Implement a traceable documentation framework linking worksite inputs to derived performance requirements
Maintain a version-controlled baseline and calibration update repository for performance targets
Establish a safety and operational policy compliance check within the performance derivation process
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a performance requirements derivation process that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets

potential_sources: Tight integration between performance derivation and documentation framework
Dependency on safety and operational policy compliance checks
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on the logical process of deriving performance requirements from mission context, which can be modularized independently from documentation and compliance checks, supporting decoupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability and accuracy of worksite mission context inputs
Risk of incomplete traceability if documentation framework is not properly integrated
Potential changes in safety and operational policies may require updates to derivation process
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating the documentation and compliance verification DPs to ensure modularity and maintainability of the performance derivation process.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for processing mission inputs and deriving performance targets, integrating data from various subsystems to produce actionable performance requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data from the worksite and vehicle sensors to inform performance calculations.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission input parameters and objectives from higher-level command systems.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts if derived performance targets indicate potential safety issues.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the performance requirements derivation logically belongs to the overall solution processing mission inputs; minimal diffusion as most data and outputs are internal or through defined interfaces.
Vehicle module is focused on physical vehicle control and status; containing the DP here would cause significant diffusion to other modules for mission inputs and performance mapping.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control aspects; performance requirements derivation is not central here and would cause high diffusion.
Safety Services may use performance targets but deriving them is broader than safety alone, causing moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the performance requirements derivation process because it integrates mission inputs and maps them to performance targets, centralizing this logic and minimizing cross-module diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related performance targets require close integration.
Risk of the module becoming too broad if other unrelated functions are also included, reducing specificity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly defines a process to derive performance requirements from worksite mission inputs, directly addressing the FR of deriving performance requirements from the mission context. The mapping is logically sound and complete.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related performance targets require close integration.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating strong cohesion and minimal scattering of the DP across modules. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives mission inputs via defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking) and outputs performance targets and safety alerts, enabling traceability and documentation of performance targets as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with minimal risk of diffusion and good alignment with the FR.

recommended_revisions: Clarify integration points with Safety Services to manage potential overlap and ensure safety-related performance targets are properly coordinated.
Monitor module scope to avoid NGHE Solution becoming too broad by segregating unrelated functions into separate modules if needed.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and feasibility. The DP is logically sound and complete, the IM boundary (NGHE Solution) shows high containment and low diffusion, and verification is feasible with clear allocation. Although there are potential overlap risks with Safety Services, these are noted for monitoring and integration clarification rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clear module boundaries and monitoring integration points is prudent, but no immediate need for further sub-FR derivation is indicated.

revise.why: Clarify integration points with Safety Services to manage potential overlap and ensure safety-related performance targets are properly coordinated.

assumptions: The current DP fully addresses the FR without missing sub-functions.
The NGHE Solution module can effectively contain the DP with minimal diffusion.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services module regarding safety-related performance targets.
evidence_needed: Documentation of integration interfaces and coordination mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Safety Services.
Verification results demonstrating traceability and correctness of performance targets derived from mission inputs.
